meta.title: The Clever Monkey — Set 1
meta.subject: English Reading
meta.description: Reading comprehension quiz for 'The Clever Monkey' — story understanding.

questions.question: Where does the story 'The Clever Monkey' take place?
questions.explanation: The story begins: 'In a jungle in West Africa, two cats found a large piece of cheese.'

questions.question: What did the two cats find in the jungle?
questions.explanation: The cats found 'a large piece of cheese.' They loved cheese more than anything.

questions.question: The monkey was sitting on the ground when he saw the two cats.
questions.explanation: The monkey was in the tree above the two cats, not on the ground.

questions.question: The monkey offered to divide the cheese into two equal parts.
questions.explanation: The monkey said, 'I will be happy to divide the cheese into two equal parts for you.'

questions.question: Why did the monkey keep eating the cheese?
options: He said the pieces were not equal and he needed to fix them
The cats asked him to eat some
He wanted to share the cheese
questions.explanation: Each time he ate some cheese, the monkey said the pieces were not equal and he needed to 'fix' them — this was his clever trick.

questions.question: The story takes place in a _______ in West Africa.
questions.hint: A tropical forest with many trees and animals
questions.explanation: 'Jungle' means a thick tropical forest. The story begins in a jungle in West Africa.

questions.question: What happened at the end of the story?
options: The cats ate all their cheese happily
The monkey gave back all the cheese
The cats were left with two tiny pieces of cheese
The monkey shared the cheese equally
questions.explanation: After all the monkey's 'fixing,' the two cats were left with only two tiny pieces of cheese.

questions.question: At the end, the two cats agreed that the pieces of cheese were the same size.
questions.explanation: At the end, the first cat said 'Your piece is bigger,' and the second cat said 'No, your piece is bigger.' They were still arguing!

questions.question: Sort each word — is it a noun (person/place/thing) or an adjective (describing word)?
questions.explanation: Jungle/mistake/fortune are things (nouns). Equal and tricky describe things (adjectives).

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'The monkey was in the tree.' He watched the cats from above.

questions.question: What does the word 'divide' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Divide' means to cut or split something into parts. The monkey 'divided' the cheese into two pieces.

questions.question: The monkey made a _______ — the pieces were not equal.
questions.explanation: 'Mistake' means an error. The monkey said 'I have made a mistake' to trick the cats.

questions.question: What is the main lesson of 'The Clever Monkey'?
options: Always share your food with others
Fighting over things can make you lose everything
Cheese is the best food in the jungle
questions.explanation: Because the cats kept arguing about who should divide the cheese, the monkey took advantage of them. The lesson is that fighting over things can cause you to lose everything.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'The cheese became smaller and smaller.' This happened as the monkey kept 'fixing' the pieces.

questions.question: The monkey was truly trying to help the cats divide the cheese fairly.
questions.explanation: The monkey was being clever and tricky. He used the cats' argument as a chance to eat the cheese for himself. He was not really helping them.
